themes: AI leverage removes experience barriers (solo founder economy)
agentic commerce & vertical tool integrations (Shopify, TradingView, eBay)
AI media stack for indie creators (continuation)

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's agents team apparently put out a 30-min walkthrough of four-layer production agent architecture — directly relevant to anyone building or speccing multi-agent systems.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues the modern computer needs to be rebuilt from scratch the way the iPhone redefined phones, not just given AI sidebars — sharp product strategy framing worth stress-testing.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Computer-use agents are now a commodity product, signaling the one-to-many GUI era will end faster than most expect — relevant to anyone thinking about distribution and interface layers.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie argues AI gives ambitious people with core skills a way to overcome historical experience requirements — a clean articulation of the solo-founder leverage thesis.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Brockman's GPT-5.5 enterprise link is the cleanest primary-source pointer to OpenAI's current enterprise positioning.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Makes the Figma-vs-Sketch distribution argument against Anthropic: OpenAI shipped a better image generator, Anthropic shipped a share button, and the market priced accordingly — worth checking if the thread has more depth.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code + Shopify AI toolkit enabling terminal-driven catalog optimization and live product page pushes is a concrete agentic commerce workflow indie sellers should know about.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: AI-generated App Store screenshots in seconds is a genuine distribution shortcut for indie devs worth five minutes to evaluate.

worthClicking.author: @minchoi
worthClicking.why: ChatGPT Images 2.0 → Seedance 2.0 → Suno 5.5 as a full 100% AI media production stack is the clearest single-post articulation of the solo creator toolchain right now.

voices.why: Posting sharp, short platform-level observations about AI's structural impact on OS and GUI paradigms — high signal-to-noise ratio and not just restating obvious takes.
Aaron Levie's occasional posts cut to the business and leverage implications of AI in a way that's useful for product strategy and sales framing.
Primary source for OpenAI product positioning; low volume but each post is a direct signal on where OpenAI is pushing enterprise narrative.
Consistently documents the practical AI creator/media stack as it evolves — useful for distribution and indie tooling tracking.

examples: aakashgupta mass-posting off-topic trivia threads (NBA floors, Caesar salad, Denzel Washington) mixed with repeated newsletter CTAs
JaynitMakwana running a GlobalGPT affiliate promo thread disguised as AI news
RoundtableSpace engagement-bait posts (GM CT, who's active, WHAT'S THE TICKER)
